tool.name: Filter collection
tool.description: using a list of identifiers from a file

param.name: input
param.label: Input Collection
param.help: A collection whose elements will be filtered.

param.name: how_filter
param.label: How should the elements to remove be determined?
param.option: Remove if identifiers are ABSENT from file
Remove if identifiers are PRESENT in file

has_text_matching.expression: ^This is a different line of text.\n$
^This is a different line of text.\n$

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Filters elements from a collection using a list of identifiers supplied in a text file.
=========== Description ===========
This tool splits a collection into two: one with elements that pass the filter and one with elements that don't. You provide a text file containing element identifiers (one per line), then choose the filtering mode:
- **Remove if identifiers are ABSENT from file** — keeps only elements whose identifiers appear in the file. Elements not listed are discarded. - **Remove if identifiers are PRESENT in file** — removes elements whose identifiers appear in the file. Elements not listed are kept.
The tool always produces **two** output collections: ``(filtered)`` containing the elements that passed, and ``(discarded)`` containing the rest. Identifiers in the file that do not match any element in the collection are silently ignored.
======== Examples ========
**Keep only listed elements** (Remove if ABSENT)
Given a collection with three elements and a text file listing ``A``, ``B``, and ``Z``::
Collection: Text file:
Dataset A A Dataset B B Dataset X Z
Elements ``A`` and ``B`` are in the file, so they are kept. ``X`` is not in the file, so it is discarded. ``Z`` is in the file but has no matching element, so it is ignored::
(filtered): Dataset A, Dataset B (discarded): Dataset X
-------
**Remove listed elements** (Remove if PRESENT)
With the same inputs but switching to PRESENT mode, the logic is inverted — elements ``A`` and ``B`` are in the file, so they are removed. ``X`` is not listed, so it is kept::
(filtered): Dataset X (discarded): Dataset A, Dataset B
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/filter_from_file.svg :alt: Filter collection from file :width: 800
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
